title: Lars Mittank: Vanished Running From Varna 📁

segments.voice: On July 8, 2014, a 28-year-old German tourist named Lars Mittank ran out of Varna Airport in Bulgaria. He had no luggage. He left his identification, his backpack, and his phone in the airport. He sprinted across the perimeter fence into a field. He has not been seen since.

Lars had been on a holiday with three friends in Golden Sands, a Bulgarian beach resort. His friends had returned to Germany earlier. Lars stayed an extra day to visit a doctor. He had developed an ear infection after a fight outside a hotel bar.

The fight had been brief. Lars and his friends had argued with a group of locals. Lars was punched. His ear was injured. A doctor in Varna prescribed medication and warned him not to fly until the inflammation cleared.

Lars called his mother Sandra Mittank from his hotel that morning. He told her he was scared. He said men were following him in the airport. He did not give specific details about the men. Sandra later described the call as the most frightening conversation of her life.

Lars went to the airport pharmacy. CCTV captured him buying medication. He sat at a chair near a column. He looked at the entrance. He looked at his phone. He stood up. He walked into the airport doctor's office for a final consultation.

Inside the medical office, the airport doctor noted Lars seemed agitated. The doctor advised him not to fly. Lars then left the office. CCTV captures him entering a men's bathroom. Inside, he is alone.

What happens next is the most analyzed sequence in the case. Lars exits the bathroom in a panic. He grabs a chair. He pushes it under a doorhandle as if to barricade something. He runs out of the airport with no luggage.
CCTV from the airport perimeter shows him climbing a chain-link fence and sprinting across an open field. He disappears into a wooded area at the edge of the airport grounds.

Bulgarian police were called. They searched the area around the airport. They found nothing. Lars's mother flew to Varna within days. She organized her own search teams. Local volunteers walked the woods. Nothing was recovered.

Here's where the case becomes its own kind of mystery. Lars's bank cards were never used. His passport was never used. His phone never logged on again. He had no known travel intentions outside of returning home.

His mother Sandra has spent over a decade investigating. She has paid for private investigators. She has worked with German federal police. She has appealed to international media. The case has been featured on multiple European missing-persons programs.

Bulgarian and German police have no shared theory. The leading possibilities — none confirmed — include a paranoid episode triggered by ear medication side effects, an actual threat from someone Lars perceived in the airport, or a mental health crisis caused by physical trauma to his head.

The medication Lars had been prescribed in Bulgaria includes side effects that, in rare cases, can cause panic, paranoia, and hallucinations. Some researchers in the case argue this explains his behavior at the airport.

Sandra Mittank does not accept the medication theory as a complete explanation. She points to her son's specific call to her — the description of being followed — as evidence that something was happening before any medication had time to take full effect.

Bulgarian police investigated the bar fight from days earlier. They identified some of the men involved. None were charged in connection with Lars's disappearance. Bulgarian authorities have stated they have no evidence of foul play.

Lars was last seen running into the woods around 11:30 a.m. local time on July 8, 2014. The temperature that day was high. The terrain near Varna Airport is difficult — partly cultivated, partly wild, with rough scrub.
Search teams over the years have used drones, dogs, and ground-penetrating radar in selected areas. Nothing has been found. The geography around Varna offers many places to hide remains.
Sandra has built a website, Find Lars, dedicated to keeping the case visible. She has documented every CCTV frame from the airport. She has translated witness statements into multiple languages.

The case has been studied by behavioral analysts in Germany. Some researchers have noted that Lars's behavior in the bathroom — pushing a chair under the door — is consistent with someone perceiving an immediate threat, regardless of whether the threat was real.

His friends, who had returned to Germany, stated they had no concerns about his mental state when they left him in Bulgaria. They described him as calm, friendly, and looking forward to coming home. None of them saw the panic that would emerge in the airport.

Lars's family in Germany has continued to advocate for the case. His mother gives interviews each July around the anniversary of his disappearance. Her message has remained consistent — she does not believe he is gone by accident.

If you visited Varna Airport on July 8, 2014, or if you have any information about Lars Mittank, the Find Lars website maintains a tip submission form in multiple languages. German federal police BKA also accept information through their missing persons line.

The case is also one of the most intensively documented missing-persons cases on YouTube and crime-research forums. Hours of CCTV footage are publicly available. The original source files remain held by Bulgarian airport authorities.
Lars would be 39 in 2025. His mother continues to look for him. Her phone is the same number it was when he called her on July 8, 2014.
